programmes.show_description: Hayley's cheeky cartoon tattoo is threatening to derail her wedding plans and Mick wants to cover an X-rated inking that's causing embarrassment to his daughter.
Charlotte Ritchie climbs a ladder with some loo roll, Mike Wozniak tries out a new catchphrase, and Lee Mack terrifies an innocent member of the public.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. Adam Riches as Sean Bean joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Shane Evans, the co-founder and chief operating officer of Massage Heights, one of the biggest massage therapy chains in North America, goes undercover in her own company.
Bruce 'Two Dogs' Bozsum, chairman of the board of a Native American gaming and entertainment empire, goes undercover to ensure that the company's casinos are thriving.
In Rugby, party queens Soroya and Jodie's unusual pie horrifies super-confident Raj. And estate agent Michael and Caroline search for flaws in Raj and Freddie's 'perfect' relationship.
In South Yorkshire, Phil and Ali host an evening fit for the A-list, there's romance in the air for Erica and Nigel, and art lovers Stephen and Kevin sculpt a sophisticated trifle.
...Selling: City living is draining the bank account for Joe and Lana, even with Joe working seven days a week. Can the bros turn their property from rags to riches?
The Wet 'n' Wild Weymouth One: Leon wants to get wed on a beach in Weymouth. But bride-to-be Kirsty wants a fairy-tale day somewhere special to her.
The Halloween Special One: Sam wants a witchy wedding in the woods. But traditional bride Ezra has her heart set on a fairy-tale event.
David Seelinger, the chairman and CEO of New Jersey-based EmpireCLS, which provides chauffeured luxury transportation, works among his staff posing as a reality show contestant.
Robert Powells, the president of Rocket Fizz Soda Pop and Candy Shops, the largest fizzy drink and sweet shop franchise in America, goes incognito among his staff.
Seascape: Gordon Ramsay visits the Seascape restaurant in the seaside village of Islip, on Long Island in New York, where owner Peter has nearly a million dollars of debt.
The Secret Garden: Gordon Ramsay visits a romantic French restaurant in California, where temperamental chef Michel Bardavid's complicated cuisine is keeping the customers away.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Kim and Pete find it hard to raise their boys in a split-level property. As they try to level up, can the bros help their home reach new heights?
Rules of En-rage-ment: The deck team band together to pull off a dazzling drag show for their guests. But a night out lands some crew members in hot water.
Greg Davies and Alex Horne set their captive band of comics humiliating missions involving cling film, fashion for insects, and face-based shape-shifting.
...Christmas Special: Jimmy Carr hosts a festive edition, with Susie Dent and Rachel Riley. Jon Richardson and Sarah Millican take on Sean Lock and David Mitchell. Plus: David O'Doherty.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Katherine Ryan and Joe Lycett take on Sean Lock and Johnny Vegas. Christopher Bliss joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Sectioning: Jeremy is forced to consider getting an old friend sectioned. And he soon realises sectioning could be a good way of getting rid of other problem people...
Shrooming: Jeremy plans to throw a secret magic mushroom party in the flat, where he hopes to seduce his ex, Big Suze.
Jay banishes Ant's backside buddy, while Glen checks out Erika's checklist. Alice blows away Cheryl's windy devil and Sketch sorts out Jason's sambuca stunt scar.
Greg Davies and Alex Horne set their captive band of comics humiliating missions involving cling film, fashion for insects, and face-based shape-shifting.
...Christmas Special: Jimmy Carr hosts a festive edition, with Susie Dent and Rachel Riley. Jon Richardson and Sarah Millican take on Sean Lock and David Mitchell. Plus: David O'Doherty.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Joe DiDomizio, the president and CEO of a travel retailer that operates news stands, bookshops and cafés in transport terminals, goes incognito among his own employees.
Greg Miller, CEO of the Larry H. Miller Group, and co-owner of National Basketball Association team the Utah Jazz, dons a disguise to go undercover among his staff.
In Somerset, Norman and Dee serve up Indian cuisine and charisma. Ashley lets down Sophie by wearing a creased shirt. And Brett and Vicky's chances become rarer as their steaks misfire.
In Cornwall, artistic Zaya and Damian serve a modern British menu, property developers Kelly and Jon show off their refurbished mansion, and Emma and Gavin host a fun-filled Cornish night.
...Selling: Nick and Julie are looking for a spacious home to accommodate every generation in their house. But first, can Drew and Jonathon mellow the yellow in this tiny place?
The Battle Re-enactment One: Fantasy fan Evan plans to wage war on his wedding day. But his emotional bride-to-be Zoe wants a low-key family affair. Will she be made to feel like a queen?
The One with All the Darts: Hapless Liam wants his greatest passion to take flight with a darts-inspired wedding. Will his arrows extravaganza hit the target?
Rick Forman, founder and CEO of New Jersey-based discount clothing superstore Forman Mills, dons a disguise to go undercover in his workforce, posing as a reality TV contestant.
Jessica Herrin, CEO of Stella & Dot, a direct sales company in the jewellery and accessories field with headquarters in San Francisco, goes undercover alongside her employees.
Lela's: In California, Gordon Ramsay visits Lela's, a fine dining restaurant run by inexperienced owner Lela and young chef Ricky. Can Gordon save them from complete disaster?
Sebastian's: Gordon Ramsay visits Sebastian's, a pizzeria in Los Angeles. The customers are scarce and the debt is mounting. Can Gordon turn the restaurant's fortunes around?
...and Selling: Brand new series: While Jonathan gets to work on Mike and Nicole's property, Drew hunts for a dog-friendly home. Let's hope he doesn't bark up the wrong tree.
Past the Boiling Point: Captain Glenn's sick of his crew not following the rules. And a heated confrontation between Gary and Chase reaches boiling point.
Jamali Maddix decorates the caravan, Sarah Kendall bites into a deep-fried kiwi fruit, and Mike Wozniak leaves Greg Davies in a state of horrified delirium.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Daisy May Cooper take on Richard Ayoade and Ivo Graham. Adam Buxton joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Joe Wilkinson assists Rachel Riley.
...Christmas Special: Jimmy Carr hosts a festive edition. Sean Lock and Bob Mortimer take on Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont. With Adam Buxton, Joe Wilkinson, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley.
Sistering: Mark's plans for a productive week off work go out the window when his sister comes to stay and, of course, Jeremy clearly has the hots for her.
Jurying: Jeremy gets called up on jury service but falls for the female defendant. Mark warns him to stop sleeping with her - it's against the law - but Jeremy ignores him.
